For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.

Pass it as the `world_model` argument of the `python` tool call, alongside `code`. That argument is the one that carries forward, so send it on every call, even when you reply with a tool call and no assistant text.
Game-tier fields (action_semantics, object_taxonomy, hud_map, win_pattern, level_log, cross_level_notes) persist across levels; level-tier fields (world_model, goal_model, recent_findings, open_questions, current_plan, strategy_log, failed_probes) reset when a level ends. Record every unproductive probe in failed_probes so it is never repeated, and tag claims VERIFIED(step N) or HYPOTHESIS.

You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The green rectangle (node 2) has grown back to 14 pixels. So clicking on node 14 toggled it back to 14 pixels. So the green rectangle (node 2) has grown back to 14 pixels. So clicking node 14 toggled it back to 14 pixels.
So the pattern is confirmed:
- C shapes toggle the green rectangle between 14 and 5 pixels.
- U shapes toggle the yellow rectangle between 23 and 14 pixels.
Now let me think about what the goal is. The game has:
- Two C shapes that toggle the green rectangle between 14 and 5 pixels.
- Two U shapes that toggle the yellow rectangle between 23 and 14 pixels.
- Two cross shapes that don't do anything meaningful.
I think the goal is to toggle all the rectangles to their "off" state (smallest size). Let me try clicking on all four shapes (both C shapes and both U shapes) to see if the level completes.
Currently:
- Green rectangle (node 2): 14 pixels (was toggled back to 14 by clicking node 14)
- Yellow rectangle (node 16): 14 pixels (was toggled to 14 by clicking node 20)
Let me try clicking on all four shapes to toggle them all to the smallest size.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Click on U shape (node 23) at row 44, col 24 to toggle yellow rectangle to 14 pixels
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 44, 'col': 24}])
</parameter>
<parameter=world_model>
{"action_semantics": {}, "obj_tax": {}, "hud_map": {"bottom_bar": "timer/progress bar at row 63"}, "win_pat": {}, "lvl_log": {}, "cross_lvl": {}, "wm": {}, "gm": {}, "rf": {}, "oq": {}, "cp": {}, "sl": {}, "fp": {}}
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
